content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

SACRAMENTO
770 L STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752

MILL VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94941
TELEPHONE (415) 389-6800

SAN FRANCISCO
650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800

July 9, 1990

Peggy Bernardi, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Bernardi:

Pursuant to our telephone conversation on June 29, 1990, I am writing to confirm the telephone advice you gave me.

I had asked whether lobbying the State Air Resources Board regarding proposed air quality guidelines for local air quality management districts constitutes "influencing legislative or administrative action" under the Political Reform Act. This question arises because earlier advice from the Commission (Advice Letter to Fred Lowell (No. A-84-291) 12/4/84) exempted local air quality management districts from the Act's lobbying provisions, and the State Board's guidelines are enforceable only by local districts.

You indicated that the State Board's guidelines come under the definition of "administrative action" in section 82002 despite the fact that they are enforced at the local district level. Lobbying the State Board regarding these guidelines is therefore reportable.

Please advise me if I have misstated your conclusion.

Sincerely,

James R. Sutton

JRS/sfr
6486.01
